next_steps: Immediate CT angiography of the chest and abdomen with aortic protocol
Transesophageal echocardiography to rule out endocarditis
Consultation with vascular surgery and cardiothoracic surgery
Continue targeted IV antibiotic therapy (cefazolin) based on susceptibility
Serial clinical monitoring for signs of aortic rupture or hemodynamic instability
Repeat CT angiography of the chest and abdomen (aortic protocol) to assess for progression or rupture of the pseudoaneurysm
Transesophageal echocardiography to evaluate for valvular vegetations and aortic root involvement
Urgent cardiothoracic and vascular surgery consultation for potential surgical intervention
MRI of the thoracic spine to differentiate spinal infection from meningiomas
Continued targeted IV antibiotics (cefazolin) with repeat blood cultures every 24-48 hours until clearance
Infectious disease consultation to optimize long-term antibiotic strategy
